sources.authority: National Health Authority, Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, Government of India
accepted_claims: Rs 5 lakh family annual health cover
SECC 2011 rural deprivation and urban occupational basis
no family size, age or gender cap
pre-existing conditions covered from day one
D1-D5 and D7 rural criteria plus automatic inclusion

facts.eligibility_dataset: SECC 2011 or applicable State beneficiary database

rural_deprivation_criteria.D1: Only one room with kucha walls and kucha roof
rural_deprivation_criteria.D2: No adult member between ages 16 to 59
rural_deprivation_criteria.D3: No adult male member between ages 16 to 59
rural_deprivation_criteria.D4: Disabled member and no able-bodied adult member
rural_deprivation_criteria.D7: Landless household deriving a major part of income from manual casual labour

urban_occupational_categories: Street vendor, cobbler, hawker or other street service provider
Construction worker, plumber, mason, labourer, painter, welder, security guard, coolie or other head-load worker
Home-based worker, artisan, handicrafts worker or tailor
Transport worker, driver, conductor, helper, cart puller or rickshaw puller
Shop worker, assistant, peon, helper, delivery assistant, attendant or waiter
Electrician, mechanic, assembler or repair worker
